data.title: Nippon Steel to raise $5.6B in subordinated loans to fund US Steel takeover
data.description:
Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) said on Wednesday it will seek to raise ¥800B (~$5.6B) through two subordinated loans to partially fund its recent $14.9B acquisition of US Steel and refinance previous loans.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) will use a ¥500B subordinated loan to partially repay a ¥2T bridge loan secured in June for the deal, and a separate ¥300B loan will refinance a previous ¥450B yen subordinated loan, according to Reuters.

The remaining 1.5T of the bridge loan will be financed through a combination of methods, based on an assessment of interest rates, market conditions and other factors, a company spokesperson said.

Following the US Steel deal, Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) debt-to-equity ratio rose to ~0.8 from 0.35 as of March 31 due to the bridge loans and a loss on the sale of its stake in a U.S. joint venture with ArcelorMittal, which it decided to sell to help win approval for the US Steel acquisition.

The company aims to reduce the debt-to-equity ratio to ~0.7 by the end of March 2026 through measures such as cash flow from earnings and asset sales, Reuters said.

data.title: STAR FASHION CULTURE prices $8M public offering of class A ordinary shares
data.description:
• STAR FASHION CULTURE (NASDAQ:STFS) announced on Thursday the pricing of its public offering of 20M Class A ordinary shares, $0.00001 par value per share, at a public offering price of $0.40 par value per share.
  • Gross proceeds, before deducting placement agent fees and other offering expenses, are expected to be $8 million.

data.title: Yunji appoints new finance head; shares up
data.description:
• Yunji (NASDAQ:YJ) senior financial director and finance head Yeqing Cui resigned (YJ) effective July 3, 2025, citing personal reasons.
  • Nan Song will succeed Cui as finance in charge.
  • Song has been with Yunji since 2015 and previously served in roles including head of financial analysis and senior finance director.
  • YJ shares up 14.1% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Brookfield Business Partners to sell some assets to seed new fund
data.description:
Brookfield Business Partners (NYSE:BBU) agreed to sell a portion of its stakes in three businesses to a new evergreen private equity strategy that targets high net-worth investors, managed by Brookfield Asset Management (NYSE:BAM), the company said Thursday.

Under the terms of the agreement, Brookfield Business Partners will sell an ~12% interest in its engineered components manufacturing operation (DexKo), an ~7% stake in its dealer software and technology services operation ("CDK Global"), and an ~5%  interest in its work access services operation (BrandSafway) to the new fund.

BBU will receive units of the new fund with an initial redemption value of ~$690M, representing an aggregate 8.6% discount to the net asset value of the interests sold. In the 18-month period after the initial closing of the new fund, expected later this year, the units are expected to be redeemed for cash at an 8.6% discount to NAV at the time of redemption. Any remaining units still outstanding after the 18-month period will be redeemable at NAV.

"The realization of these partial interests, at a value that is accretive to the trading price of our units and shares, enables Brookfield Business Partners (NYSE:BBU) to continue to accelerate the return of capital under current and future buyback programs, reinvest in the growth of its business and reduce corporate leverage," said Brookfield Business Partners CEO Anuj Ranjan.

A joint independent committee comprising independent directors of Brookfield Business Partners (BBU) retained an independent financial advisor and external legal counsel to assist with its review of the transaction. The committee received a fairness opinion from its independent financial advisor, and after consulting with the advisors determined that the transaction is fair and in the best interests of the company.

The sale is expected to be completed on July 4, 2025.

Brookfield Business Partners (BBU) stock edged up 0.1%, and Brookfield Asset Management  (NYSE:BAM)  stock gained 0.5% in premarket trading.

data.title: KORE joins Russell Microcap index
data.description:
• Kore Group Holdings (NYSE:KORE) has been added to the Russell Microcap Index following the 2025 Russell indexes reconstitution, effective after the U.S. market opened on June 30. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Elanco in licensing pact with Australia’s Neurizon
data.description:
Australia-based biotech Neurizon Therapeutics (OTCPK:PAAFF) has inked an exclusive global licensing agreement with Elanco Animal Health (NYSE:ELAN) to obtain rights for monepantel, the active pharmaceutical ingredient in its lead asset NUZ-001.

Neurizon expects the deal to accelerate the development of NUZ-001, which is currently undergoing trials in humans for neurodegenerative diseases, including amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

As part of the agreement, Neurizon (OTCPK:PAAFF) will receive exclusive access to data and intellectual property rights related to monepantel by paying an undisclosed upfront licensing fee to the U.S. veterinary product firm.

Elanco (NYSE:ELAN) will also be eligible for $9.75M and $5.2M in development milestone payments for initial and subsequent licensed products, indications, etc., respectively. The company’s sales-based milestone payments are expected to reach up to $65M.

Additionally, there will be tiered single-digit royalties on global net sales of licensed products. With the companies focused on closing a supply agreement, a follow-up announcement on the deal is expected in H2 2025.

data.title: Broadridge to acquire European fund distribution firm Acolin, terms undisclosed
data.description:
Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) announced on Thursday an acquisition of the European cross-border fund distribution and regulatory services provider, Acolin.

Shares were -0.20% pre-market to $237.55.

The terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The acquisition is expected to facilitate Broadridge's expansion plans in Europe.

The transaction is expected to close in the first half of the 2026 fiscal year, and is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial results.

data.title: Datadog being added to S&P 500 is 'validation moment' for company: Wedbush
data.description:
The inclusion of Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) to the S&P 500 (SP500) is a “validation moment” for the observability software company, Wedbush Securities said.

Shares rose 9.3% in premarket trading on Thursday.

“While the investment community largely expected Robinhood or AppLovin to join the index, DDOG was selected over these names as the company’s platform continues to gain momentum within the observability space, especially with AI front and center,” Wedbush analysts said in a note to clients.

They continued: “With the influx of agentic AI sidekicks, the company has focused intently on innovating through Bits AI as DDOG finds new opportunities to streamline the developer experience with products that leverage features like 24/7 background scanning to alert developers of issues, automated suggestions for bug fixes with summaries for traceability, and innovative stat monitoring methods.”

The investment firm, which raised its price target on Datadog to $170 from $140, added that Datadog is “well positioned” to keep gaining share in observability and its recent partnership with OpenAI is likely to compliment the agentic AI trends going across the globe.

Datadog will replace Juniper Networks after it was removed due to Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) completing its acquisition of the networking products provider. The change becomes effective on July 9.

data.title: FedEx gains after BNP Paribas flips from being a bear to a bull
data.description:
FedEx Corporation (NYSE:FDX) gained in early trading after BNP Paribas flipped from being a bear on the shipping stock to being a bull. Analyst Hugo Watkins and his team think the stock is oversold considering FedEx (NYSE:FDX) is outperforming on a relative basis on both volume and yield growth

BNP Paribas thinks the relative operational outperformance to rival United Parcel Service (UPS) will continue because UPS is facing company-specific headwinds.

Despite the U.S. domestic parcel market being competitive and Amazon (AMZN) continuing to present a major challenge to incumbents, FedEx (FDX) is seen stringing together some solid earnings reports. 

The firm moved to an Outperform rating on FedEx (FDX) after having it set at Underperform and assigned a new price target of $270. 

The Wall Street analyst scorecard on FedEx (FDX) shows 21 Buy-equivalent ratings, 8 Hold-equivalent ratings, and 3 Sell-equivalent ratings on the books. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Hold rating on the stock.

Shares of FedEx (FDX) rose 1.3% in premarket action to $242.33.

data.title: BlackRock considers selling Saudi Aramco gas pipelines stake - report
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), the giant asset manager, is weighing the sale of its interest in the leasing rights to Saudi Aramco's (ARMCO) natural gas pipeline network back to the energy behemoth, according to a media report published on Thursday.

The two companies are in talks for a potential transaction, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) will also consider other options for the stake if the talks don't result in an agreement, according ot some of the people.

BlackRock (BLK) acquired the stake in the assets in 2021, which is likely to be valued at billions of dollars, the report said. A group led by BlackRock (BLK) had invested $15.5B to buy 49% of the entity that holds leasing rights over the pipelines.

In a similar transaction that year, Aramco (ARMCO) sold a $12.4B stake related to its oil pipelines, the article said. The two deals, among the energy giant's largest sales, reflect Saudi Arabia's shift to sell assets to develop new industries such as electric vehicles and artificial intelligence.

No final decisions have been made on BlackRock's (BLK) stake in the natural gas pipeline leasing rights, and there's no guarantee the talks will lead to a deal, Bloomberg said.
